events.description: Tesla officially announced a 5-for-1 stock split in the form of a stock dividend to make shares more accessible to employees and retail investors.
The record date was reached, establishing which shareholders were entitled to receive four additional shares for every one share held.
events.quantitative_detail: 4 additional shares for every 1 share held

events.description: The distribution date occurred, with the additional shares being issued to shareholders after the close of trading.
Tesla stock began trading on a split-adjusted basis, significantly lowering the per-share price for market participants.

stakeholders.name: Tesla Board of Directors
stakeholders.position: The Board declared the 5-for-1 stock split with the explicit purpose of making stock ownership more accessible to employees and investors.

outcome_description: The 5-for-1 stock split was met with overwhelming market enthusiasm, resulting in a significant surge in Tesla's share price as retail investors gained easier access to the stock. The move was widely viewed as a successful strategic effort to increase liquidity and broaden the company's shareholder base.
outcome_source: Market sentiment analysis and trading volume data reported by Business Insider and financial news outlets during the August 2020 period (Confidence < 0.5)
